nodes.name: Sticky Note7

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!

### This n8n templates demonstrates how to build a simple subscriber service entirely in n8n using n8n forms as a frontend, n8n generally as the backend and Airtable as the storage layer.

This template in particular shows a fully automated service to send automated messages containing facts about a topic the user requested for.

### How it works
* An n8n form is setup up to allow users to subscribe with a desired topic and interval of which to recieve messages via n8n forms which is then added to the Airtable.
* A scheduled trigger is executed every morning and searches for subscribers to send messages for based on their desired intervals.
* Once found, Subscribers are sent to a subworkflow which performs the text content generation via an AI agent and also uses a vision model to generate an image.
* Both are attached to an email which is sent to the subscriber. This email also includes an unsubscribe link.
* The unsubscribe flow works similarly via n8n form interface which when submitted disables further scheduled emails to the user.

## How to use
* Make a copy of sample Airtable here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
* Make sure the workflow is "activated" and the forms are available and reachable by your audience.


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

parameters.content:
### 4. Using Subworkflows to run executions concurrently
This configuration is desired when sequential execution is slow and unnecessary. Also if one email fails, it doesn't fail the execution for everyone else.

### 1. Subscribe flow
Use a form to allow users to subscribe to the service.

### 2. Unsubscribe flow
* Uses Form's pre-fill field feature to identify user
* Doesn't use "email" as identifier so you can't unsubscribe others

### 3. Scheduled Trigger
* Runs every day at 9am
* Handles all 3 frequency types
* Send emails concurrently

### 5. Use Execution Data to Filter Logs
If you've registered for community+ or are on n8n cloud, best practice is to use execution node to allow filtering of execution logs.

### 6. Use AI to Generate Factoid and Image
Use an AI agent to automate the generation of factoids as requested by the user. This is a simple example but we recommend a adding a unique touch to stand out from the crowd!

### 7. Send Email to User
Finally, send a message to the user with both text and image.
Log the event in the Airtable for later analysis if required.

![Screenshot of email result]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## Try It Out!

### This n8n templates demonstrates how to build a simple subscriber service entirely in n8n using n8n forms as a frontend, n8n generally as the backend and Airtable as the storage layer.

This template in particular shows a fully automated service to send automated messages containing facts about a topic the user requested for.

### How it works
* An n8n form is setup up to allow users to subscribe with a desired topic and interval of which to recieve messages via n8n forms which is then added to the Airtable.
* A scheduled trigger is executed every morning and searches for subscribers to send messages for based on their desired intervals.
* Once found, Subscribers are sent to a subworkflow which performs the text content generation via an AI agent and also uses a vision model to generate an image.
* Both are attached to an email which is sent to the subscriber. This email also includes an unsubscribe link.
* The unsubscribe flow works similarly via n8n form interface which when submitted disables further scheduled emails to the user.

## How to use
* Make a copy of sample Airtable here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
* Make sure the workflow is "activated" and the forms are available and reachable by your audience.


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: airtable Token  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Execute Workflow Trigger".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Unsubscribe Form".
Verify the settings of the trigger node "Subscribe Form".
Open the "Search daily" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search weekly" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "confirmation email1" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Execute Workflow" node (n8n-nodes-base.executeWorkflow) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Log Last Sent" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Search surprise" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Should Send = True" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Create Subscriber" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update Subscriber" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Execution Data" node (n8n-nodes-base.executionData) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Window Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wikipedia" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Content Generation Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Groq Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate Image" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Resize Image" node (n8n-nodes-base.editImage) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Send Message" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmail) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Create Event".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set Email Vars".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
